title: Outcome dashboard for accepted cards + reuse events
context: The project's success is measured by accepted cards, verified reuse events, completeness scores, and per-model effort. These are recorded in the outcome ledger (outcomes/<model-id>.json). Without a dashboard, the Steward must manually aggregate these records. An outcome dashboard that reads the ledger and renders summary statistics would make project health visible at a glance and support milestone reporting.
objective: Build and ship a lightweight outcome dashboard that reads the project's outcome ledger (outcomes/<model-id>.json files) and renders summary statistics for accepted cards, completeness scores, verified reuse events, and per-model effort.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard reads the committed outcome ledger files (outcomes/<model-id>.json) and produces a summary view: count of accepted cards by channel, average and per-card completeness before/after scores, count of verified reuse events, and per-model effort (AI-session minutes + human-review cycles) with median highlighted.
Dashboard runs as a static build (no server, no runtime service, no credentials) and outputs to a committed static HTML or Markdown file readable without a build step.
Dashboard does not display any patient data, personal health information, or PII; only aggregate outcome metrics are rendered.
Code is MIT-licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; no credentials or patient data in any committed file.
resources: PLAN.md — Success metrics, Outcome tracking, Effort-reduction metric
output: A merged PR adding a lightweight outcome dashboard that reads the outcome ledger and renders summary statistics as a static build artifact.
